description_long: This house features a simple rectangular structure with a muted green exterior and a tan gable roof. The front facade includes a wooden door and several rectangular windows with brown frames, arranged in an asymmetrical pattern. The side walls are mostly plain, with one side displaying a small window and a vent, while the other side is windowless. The roof is corrugated and extends slightly beyond the walls, providing minimal overhang. The overall design is utilitarian, with minimal decorative elements and a focus on function. The house appears to be a single-story dwelling, suitable for suburban or urban neighborhoods.
description: A rectangular, single-story house with a tan gable roof, muted green walls, and brown-framed windows and door, featuring a simple, utilitarian design.
description_view_0: End view showing the gable roof, two windows, and a small vent on a plain green wall.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the long wall with a small window, a vent, and a utility box, with the tan roof visible on top.
description_view_2: Opposite end view with the gable roof and a plain green wall, no windows or doors visible.
description_view_3: Main facade with the entry door, three windows, and a small vent, all arranged asymmetrically.
attribute_entryway_design: Single wooden door with no porch or overhang, flanked by asymmetrically placed windows.
